definition: A self-executing contract with the terms of the agreement directly written into lines of code, residing and running on a blockchain network.
A comprehensive, digital record for a product, aggregating crucial information throughout its lifecycle, from cultivation to logistics.
A South African organization responsible for ensuring that all perishable products intended for export comply with prescribed quality, food safety, and phytosanitary standards.

term: Department of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries (DAFF)
definition: The South African government department responsible for agricultural policy, food security, and phytosanitary regulations.
The revenue service of South Africa, responsible for tax collection and customs management.

A decentralized, distributed, and often public, digital ledger that is used to record transactions across many computers so that any involved record cannot be altered retroactively without the alteration of all subsequent blocks and the consensus of the network.
A decentralized, open-source blockchain with smart contract functionality, known for its large developer community and established ecosystem.
A permissioned blockchain framework designed for enterprise-grade applications, offering modular architecture for private, consortium-based networks.
A serverless execution environment at the edge of Cloudflare's global network, used for running JavaScript code and implementing distributed ledger-like functionalities.
